vulnerabilities.title: Prototype Pollution in hoek
vulnerabilities.description:
Versions of `hoek` prior to 4.2.1 and 5.0.3 are vulnerable to prototype pollution.

The `merge` function, and the `applyToDefaults` and `applyToDefaultsWithShallow` functions which leverage `merge` behind the scenes, are vulnerable to a prototype pollution attack when provided an _unvalidated_ payload created from a JSON string containing the `__proto__` property.

This can be demonstrated like so:

```javascript
var Hoek = require('hoek');
var malicious_payload = '{"__proto__":{"oops":"It works

vulnerabilities.title: hoek subject to prototype pollution via the clone function.
vulnerabilities.description: hoek versions prior to 8.5.1, and 9.x prior to 9.0.3 are vulnerable to prototype pollution in the clone function. If an object with the __proto__ key is passed to clone() the key is converted to a prototype. This issue has been patched in version 9.0.3, and backported to 8.5.1.
